design_notes: Three scripted scenarios chosen to span three different *types* of operational stress.
S1 = steady-state surfacing (no crisis; system finds non-obvious latent risk).
S2 = predictable seasonal disruption with full cascade (the demo headliner).
S3 = compound failure across a different dimension (vehicle reliability) showing the platform isn't a one-trick weather model.
Each scenario has a 90-day window with daily state computed for focal posts and SKUs.
Cascade chains are explicit: disruption -> route impact -> alternate viability -> downstream stockout -> resupply recommendation.
Resupply options (3 per critical event) span cost / time / risk space so the operator chooses, the system doesn't decide.

headcount_assumptions.rationale: Real headcount per post is operationally sensitive even at the unclassified level. Scenario-internal notional headcounts used here are illustrative central estimates for post type and operational tempo.

demo_starting_inventory_assumptions.rationale: Demo Day-0 inventory is set so each scenario produces operationally interesting horizons (~30-45 days to stockout). Real Army stocking levels follow War Wastage Reserves and seasonal pre-stocking doctrine that are not public.
demo_starting_inventory_assumptions.approach: Inventory at each focal post is set such that, under normal operations, post would not run out. Disruption events compress the horizon.

math_methodology.daily_projection: stock_today = stock_yesterday - daily_burn(post, sku, season, terrain) + arrivals(post, sku, day) - losses(spoilage, freeze, etc.)
math_methodology.confidence_interval: 10/50/90 percentile bands on daily_burn estimates; widens with horizon
math_methodology.resupply_cost: tonnes * cost_per_tonne_km * km, summed across edges in the plan
math_methodology.resupply_time: max segment transit + handling time at each node
math_methodology.resupply_risk: P[plan_completes] = product of segment_availability_P and vehicle_availability_P

_meta.demo_narrative_use: The UI scrubs through the 90-day window. At each disruption event, the dashboard re-computes affected posts and pops the resupply-options card. The Brigadier picks one; the system shows lineage back to model and source data.

scenarios.title: Normal operations baseline (late summer)
scenarios.narrative_one_liner: No crisis. System surfaces a latent vehicle-reliability risk on the DBO axis that's invisible to the operator at Day 0.
scenarios.weather_assumption: Typical late-summer conditions. All passes open. IMD data baseline.

disruption_events.description: EME records show Stallion serial-batch 2019/05 (8 vehicles in the 12-truck DBO convoy pool) approaching 18-month service interval simultaneously. None deadlined yet, but failure-rate prior spikes from 1.8/1000vkm to 4.5/1000vkm.
disruption_events.data_source: vehicles.json reliability_prior on summer_road_unpaved + maintenance interval crossing
disruption_events.operator_visibility_without_bastion: low — quartermaster sees individual vehicle service dates, not aggregate convoy reliability

disruption_events.description: 3-day rain event blocks Shyok river crossings on DSDBO road. Convoys delay 2 days.
disruption_events.data_source: IMD historical late-summer Shyok-crossing closure pattern

disruption_events.description: Pre-winter stockpiling order from MGS branch: bring DBO winter kerosene reserve to 30,000L by Day 75. Demand triples for 23 days.
disruption_events.data_source: Standard pre-winter doctrine; MGS directive cycle is publicly documented

focal_post_projection.kerosene_burn_calc: 150 hc x 2.4 L/soldier/day (ECC) x 1.0 winter_uplift (off-season) = 360 L/day

day_52.note: MGS pre-winter directive issued, demand triples

day_75.note: surge convoys completed; reserve at target

system_recommendation_at_day_18.trigger: Vehicle reliability prior crosses threshold for DBO convoy pool
system_recommendation_at_day_18.non_obvious_insight: 8 of 12 DBO-axis Stallions hitting service interval in a 6-week window. Probability that 3+ simultaneously deadline before Day 75 surge: 67%.
system_recommendation_at_day_18.recommended_action: Stagger preventive maintenance over Days 19-40 to avoid concurrent deadlining. Coordinate with EME workshop at Karu to ensure 9-vehicle minimum availability throughout Day 52-75 surge window.
system_recommendation_at_day_18.operator_value: This insight is invisible without aggregating EME service records against forecast demand. Surfacing it 17 days before any vehicle actually fails is the platform's value proposition in a single example.

scenarios.title: Zoji La closes early; Manali already shut; Kargil cluster cascades
scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Late October blizzard closes Zoji La 3 weeks earlier than expected. Manali road already shut. Kargil cluster has 27 days of kerosene. Air bridge is the only inbound option but already at capacity for Leh resupply.
scenarios.weather_assumption: Severe early-winter onset. IMD anomaly: blizzard event on Day 5 brings 1.2m snow at Zoji La, closing it 3 weeks before historical median closure.

starting_state.closed_routes_reason: Manali-Leh closes ~mid-Oct annually due to Baralacha and Taglang La snow. Closed since Day -8 (Oct 7).

POST-013_Kargil_HQ.comment: Kargil cluster aggregate stock: 60kL serves Kargil HQ + Drass + Mushkoh + Tiger Hill. Set to produce ~27-day horizon for demo tension.

disruption_events.description: Blizzard at Zoji La. 1.2m snow in 36 hours. BRO declares pass closed. Z-Morh tunnel still under construction; no all-weather alternate.
disruption_events.data_source: Modelled on IMD historical anomaly events; closure mechanism documented in BRO Beacon bulletins

route_impacts.implication: Kargil cluster is now fed only via Leh, which itself is now fed only by air

disruption_events.description: System recognizes Kargil cluster has 27 days of kerosene at current burn. Posts a critical alert.
disruption_events.operator_visibility_without_bastion: low — aggregating across 4 posts and projecting against actual winter consumption rates is not a manual exercise
disruption_events.non_obvious_insight: If Tiger Hill OP is excluded from cluster aggregate (because foot-only resupply means it operates on its own pre-positioned stock), Kargil HQ + Drass + Mushkoh runs out on Day 30. Tiger Hill independently has ~50 days because it pre-stocks for full winter.

disruption_events.description: Heavy snow at Drass adds 30% local heating demand for 2 weeks. Cluster burn rises from 2,189 L/day to 2,615 L/day.
disruption_events.data_source: IMD Drass historical winter cold-snap pattern
disruption_events.operator_visibility_without_bastion: medium — local commander reports increased demand but doesn't aggregate cluster-wide

day_0.note: 27 days assumes baseline burn; cluster is healthy

day_5.note: Zoji La closes; alternate analysis triggered

day_12.note: system raises alert; recommends air resupply via Leh-Kargil road

day_18.note: Drass cold-snap adds 426 L/day demand; horizon collapses

day_22.note: without resupply by Day 26, posts begin rationing

day_26.note: 55kL emergency airlift completes via Leh-Kargil road; reserve restored

resupply_options_at_day_12_alert.context: Kargil cluster needs ~55kL kerosene delivered by Day 25. Both road LoCs from Srinagar are closed. Must originate from air bridge to Leh, then truck Leh-Kargil.

options.name: Heavy-air priority lift
options.plan: 5x C-17 Hindon-Leh sorties + 1x Stallion convoy Leh-Kargil
options.cost_breakdown: C-17 air at 8.5 INR/tonne-km baseline x 720km x 55t + truck Leh-Kargil at 0.45 baseline x 200km x 55t
options.risk_drivers: C-17 weather availability at Leh (winter clear-weather availability ~0.75); Leh-Kargil road ice in mid-Oct
options.why_recommended: Fastest. Highest payload-per-sortie. Frees Mi-17 fleet for forward ALG resupply. But: pulls C-17 from competing Leh-Corps-replenishment task.

options.name: Distributed Mi-17 lift
options.plan: 12x Mi-17 Hindon-Leh sorties + 1x Stallion convoy Leh-Kargil
options.cost_breakdown: Mi-17 at 9.0 INR/tonne-km x 720km x 55t + truck portion same as A
options.risk_drivers: Mi-17 winter clear-weather availability ~0.78 but more airframes available; spread across 12 sorties = lower per-sortie criticality
options.why_recommended: Higher reliability than C-17 path because losing 1 Mi-17 sortie costs 8% of plan vs 20% for C-17. Costlier but safer when air conditions are marginal.

options.name: Reduce demand + stretched lift
options.plan: Ration kerosene to 75% at Kargil HQ + Drass for 14 days (saves 5.4kL); 8x C-17 sorties over 12 days; 2 truck convoys
options.cost_breakdown: Lower volume (50t vs 55t) plus stretched timeline reduces priority surcharge
options.risk_drivers: Longest weather window across multiple sortie days = most robust
options.why_recommended: Cheapest. Highest reliability. Cost: 14 days of reduced heating at Kargil HQ + Drass means soldier comfort impact and modest health risk. Acceptable at HA but not at SHA/ECC posts (which keep full ration). Only viable if Brigadier accepts comfort tradeoff.

resupply_options_at_day_12_alert.tradeoff_summary: Speed vs reliability vs human comfort. A is fastest but riskiest. C is cheapest and most reliable but trades soldier comfort. B is the middle path. The system does not pick — the Brigadier picks.

scenarios.title: Three Stallions deadline at Karu the week the DBO surge convoy departs
scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Mid-September, planned 12-Stallion convoy to DBO is pre-winter kerosene + spares lift. Three Stallions deadline at Karu workshop in Days 8-10. Remaining 9 trucks at DBO altitude derate (5,065m) lift 55t instead of planned 73t. Below threshold for DBO winter readiness.
scenarios.weather_assumption: Typical late-summer-early-autumn conditions. All passes still open. No weather disruption — this scenario tests the vehicle-reliability dimension specifically.

planned_dbo_surge_convoy.primary_cargo: kerosene 45kL (36t) + diesel 12kL (10t) + spares + rations + ammunition + cold-weather kit

disruption_events.description: Stallion serial 19/05/02 deadlined at Karu workshop — clutch failure during pre-convoy inspection.
disruption_events.data_source: vehicles.json reliability prior; serial batch was flagged in S1 as approaching service interval

disruption_events.description: Stallion serial 19/05/04 deadlined at Karu — engine compression test fail.
Stallion serial 19/05/07 deadlined at Karu — gearbox seizure during loading.
disruption_events.fleet_impact: 9 of 12 trucks available; convoy capacity drops to 55.4t (from 73.8t planned)

focal_post_projection.season: transitioning summer to winter (assumed Day 30 onwards = winter rates)

day_16.note: Convoy delivered short. Winter stockpile target missed by 28kL.

day_30.note: winter rates engaged; horizon adequate but below winter doctrine target

day_45.note: approaching DSDBO road closure window; remaining road resupply opportunities limited

day_60.note: DSDBO marginal; air bridge dependence rising

day_75_with_remediation.note: if remediation plan executed (see resupply options), reaches winter-safe levels

resupply_options_at_day_10_alert.context: Three Stallions deadlined at Karu; planned DBO convoy capacity dropped 25%. Need to make up 18.4 tonnes of cargo before DSDBO road closes (~Day 60-75 typical).

options.name: Borrow Stallions from Pangong-Chushul tasking
options.plan: Pull 3x Stallions from POST-005 Chushul resupply pool (which has stocked light for the season); restore 12-truck convoy; depart Day 14 as planned
options.cost_note: no incremental cost — uses already-funded fleet differently
options.risk_drivers: Pangong-Chushul cluster now under-resourced; if a contingency emerges there in next 30 days, no slack. Low probability but high consequence if it hits.
options.why_recommended: Fastest. No incremental cost. Best if Pangong-Chushul forecast is quiet. Worst if it isn't.

options.name: Two-trip plan — original + makeup convoy
options.plan: 9-truck convoy departs Day 14 as planned (55.4t); second 4-truck convoy of expedited spares + makeup load departs Day 28 (after first 3 deadlined trucks return to service at Karu workshop, ~Day 25)
options.cost_breakdown: Second convoy round trip Karu-DBO 510km x 4 trucks x 1.9 INR/tonne-km baseline x 23.6t
options.risk_drivers: DSDBO road open through Day 28 has 92% historical probability in mid-September (IMD). EME workshop turnaround on 3 deadlined trucks: 90% within 18 days.
options.why_recommended: Compromise option. Two convoy windows hedge against single-event risk. Modest cost. No fleet displacement from other taskings.

options.name: Heavy-air supplement
options.plan: 9-truck convoy as planned + 4x Mi-17 sorties Leh-DBO ALG carrying 4t each (high-priority kerosene + spares only)
options.cost_breakdown: Mi-17 at 9.0 INR/tonne-km x 100km Leh-DBO x 16t (after derate) + truck convoy normal
options.risk_drivers: Mi-17 summer clear-weather availability 0.88; multiple sorties give resilience
options.why_recommended: Highest reliability. Spreads risk across two delivery modes. Costliest.

resupply_options_at_day_10_alert.tradeoff_summary: A is risky (depletes elsewhere), B is balanced, C is robust. Cost ratio A:B:C is 0:1:2.3. Reliability ratio is 0.65:0.82:0.88. Different Brigadiers will choose differently — that's the point.

cross_scenario_demo_value: S1 surfaces a vehicle-reliability insight invisible without aggregation; S3 dramatizes the same risk realized.
S2 demonstrates the cascade chain across 3+ depths (weather -> route -> alternate -> downstream stockout -> resupply choice).
All three end with the operator choosing among 3 options, not the system deciding. Decision-support, not decision-replacement.
Kerosene appears in 2 of 3 as the focal SKU because it's genuinely the dominant SHA/ECC consumable; this is a feature, not repetition.
